title: Irish Fairy Tales
description: Irish Fairy Tales — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Irish Fairy Tales" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: Aillen mac Midna
memes.description: A fairy being from Shí Finnachy who came each Samhain to burn Tara with his fiery breath, until slain by the young Fionn mac Cumhaill.

memes.label: "Allen"
memes.description: "Allen is the hill-fort and great plain in Leinster that serves as the chief seat and gathering-place of the Fianna, where Fionn mac Uail holds court and where many of the warrior-band's adventures begin and end."

memes.label: "The Little Brawl at Allen — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "“I think,” said Cairell Whiteskin, “that although judgement was given"

memes.label: "The Little Brawl at Allen — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "This version of the death of Uail is not correct. Also Cnocha is not in"

memes.label: "The Little Brawl at Allen — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "AT the end of a fortnight Fionn and Goll and the chief men of the Fianna"

memes.label: "Angus Og"
memes.description: "Angus Og is the Tuatha De Danann god of love and youth, chief and deity of the Brugh of the Boyne in Ulster, one of the three great burial places of Ireland."

memes.label: "Art mac Conn"
memes.description: "Art mac Conn, son of Conn of the Hundred Battles, is the heir to the High Kingship of Ireland who rules as regent during Conn's year-long Faery quest and later succeeds him as Ard-Ri."

memes.label: Bardic Tradition
memes.description: The bardic and fili tradition in early Irish society—the class of learned poet-seers who preserved law, genealogy, and lore through verse, and for whom poetry was the highest expression of wisdom.

memes.label: Battle of Cnocha
memes.description: The Battle of Cnocha, fought in Lochlann, where Uail mac Baiscne — father of Fionn mac Uail and captain of the Fianna — fought and died, leading to the breaking of Clann Baiscne and Fionn's secret upbringing in the wilderness.

memes.label: "Battle of Ventry Harbour"
memes.description: "The Battle of Ventry Harbour is the great defensive battle of the Fenian Cycle in which Fionn mac Uail and the Fianna repelled the invasion of Ireland by the King of the World — the proving ground of the Fianna's martial prowess and a turning point in the cycle's heroic age."

memes.label: "Becfola"
memes.description: "Becfola, called 'the Dowerless,' is the mysterious queen who flees Tara on a strange journey through the Otherworld, confronting enchantment, violence, and the hidden truths of the kingdom she left behind."

memes.label: "The Wooing of Becfola — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "We do not know where Becfola came from. Nor do we know for certain where"

memes.label: "The Wooing of Becfola — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "They were married in a haste which equalled the king’s desire; and as he"

memes.label: "The Wooing of Becfola — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "But after some time the moon arose and the wolves went away, for their"

memes.description: "IT was so early that not even a bird was yet awake, and the dull grey"

memes.label: "The Wooing of Becfola — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "In the morning as they sat at breakfast four clerics were announced, and"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin"
memes.description: "Becuma Cneisgel, or Becuma of the White Skin, is a sídhe woman banished from the Otherworld for a transgression, who becomes the wife of Conn of the Hundred Battles and brings blight upon Ireland until her exile."

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "There are more worlds than one, and in many ways they are unlike each"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "Conn the Hundred Fighter, Ard-Ri’ of Ireland, was in the lowest spirits"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "Being a king he had authority to ask questions. Conn asked her,"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "It is part of the education of a prince to be a good chess player, and"

memes.description: "The High King did not know where exactly he should look for such a"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin — CHAPTER VI"
memes.description: "Having gone some distance by these pleasant ways he saw a shapely house"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin — CHAPTER VII"
memes.description: "On reaching the palace Conn called his magicians and poets to a council"

memes.description: "There are good and evil people in this and in every other world, and the"

memes.description: "Art, as his father had done before him, set out for the Many-Coloured"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin — CHAPTER X"
memes.description: "He went forward in his light bark, and at some moment found that he"

memes.label: "Becuma of the White Skin (Tale)"
memes.description: "Becuma of the White Skin is the ninth tale in James Stephens' Irish Fairy Tales, telling the story of Becuma Cneisgel, a woman of the Tuatha Dé Danann banished from the Otherworld who brings blight upon Ireland until her exile."

memes.label: "Ben Edair"
memes.description: "Ben Edair (modern Howth Head) is the coastal promontory near Dublin from which heroes embark on sea journeys to Faery and to which they return bearing wonders."

memes.label: Ben Gulbain
memes.description: Ben Gulbain (modern Benbulbin in Co. Sligo) is a prominent mountain and traditional hunting ground of the Fianna in Irish mythology.

memes.label: "Bovmall"
memes.description: "Bovmall is a druid-woman, sister of Uail and aunt of Fionn, who together with Lia Luachra fostered the infant Fionn in the woods of Slieve Bloom after his father's death. She was married to Fiacuil mac Cona."

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "Fionn pronounce Fewn to rhyme with “tune” got his first training among"

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "Were his nurses comely or harsh-looking? Fionn would not know. This was"

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "THE women druids, his guardians, belonged to his father’s people."

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "He was given good training by the women in running and leaping and"

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "ONE day his guardians were agitated: they held confabulations at which"

memes.description: "But Fionn was gone. He was away, bound with his band of poets for the"

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER VII"
memes.description: "A new life for Fionn in the robber’s den that was hidden in a vast cold"

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER VIII"

memes.label: "The Boyhood of Fionn — CHAPTER XIV"
memes.description: "He listened to the retreating footsteps until they could be heard no"

memes.label: "Boyne"
memes.description: "The Boyne is a sacred river in Ulster, home to Finegas the poet, site of the Salmon of Knowledge, and location of the Brugh — one of the three great burial-places of ancient Ireland."

memes.label: "Bran"
memes.description: "Bran was one of Fionn mac Uail's two legendary wolfhounds, born of a Sidhe enchantment alongside Sceolan, and Fionn's most trusted hunting companion throughout the Fenian tales."

memes.label: Bran and Sceólan
memes.description: The two legendary wolfhounds of Fionn mac Uail, born from the transformation of Tuiren, Fionn's aunt, and known for their unmatched speed, human-like loyalty, and supernatural origins.

memes.label: "The Birth of Bran — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "There are people who do not like dogs a bit--they are usually women--but"

memes.label: "The Birth of Bran — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "When Iollan and Tuiren were married they went to Ulster, and they lived"

memes.label: "The Birth of Bran — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "As they paced along Uct Dealv railed bitterly against the hound, and"

memes.label: "The Birth of Bran — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "On the following day Fergus called his servant."

memes.label: "The Birth of Bran — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "In course of time the news came to Fionn that his mother’s sister"

memes.label: "Branduv"
memes.description: "Branduv mac Echach, King of Leinster, was a rival of Mongan whose obsessive love for Duv Laca drove the central conflict of Mongan's Frenzy."

relations.label: Duv Laca of the White Hand

memes.label: Brawl at Allen
memes.description: A violent confrontation at the Fianna's stronghold of Allen of Leinster, beginning as a quarrel during a feast and escalating into a full battle involving eleven hundred of Fionn's warriors.

memes.label: "Cael of the Iron"
memes.description: "Cael of the Iron is a champion of the Fianna of Ireland who appears in *The Carl of the Drab Coat* as a boastful runner challenged by the supernatural Carl to a punishing foot-race across the hills of Ireland."

memes.label: "Caelte mac Rónán"
memes.description: "Caelte mac Rónán was a Fenian warrior of the Fianna, renowned as the swiftest runner in Ireland, and a close companion of Fionn mac Uail."

memes.label: "Cairide"
memes.description: "Cairide was the story-teller and chronicler of the court of Mongan, King of Ulster. He is the figure to whom we owe the preservation of Mongan's supernatural adventures — the scribe who first committed to vellum the account of Mongan's journey into Faery, providing the narrative frame that makes 'Mongan's Frenzy' a story-within-a-story of remarkable sophistication."

memes.label: "Cairill"
memes.description: "Cairill was King of Ulster in the early Christian period. His fisherman's capture of Tuan mac Cairill in salmon form, and Tuan's subsequent recounting of Irish history at Cairill's court, provides the narrative frame for the oldest tale in the collection."

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "One day something happened to Fionn, the son of Uail; that is, he"

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "He had not gone a great distance from Ben Edair when he came to"

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "When they came among the host the men of Ireland gathered about the vast"

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "“Cael, my pulse,” said the Carl, “we had better build a house or a hut"

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "At the end of an hour the Carl awoke. He ate the second half of the"

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER VI"
memes.description: "It would be hard to tell of the terror that was in Fionn’s breast and"

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER VII"
memes.description: "“What do you see?” Fionn demanded of the watcher."

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat — CHAPTER VIII"
memes.description: "The Carl of the Drab Coat came bumping and stumping and clumping into"

relations.label: The Carl of the Drab Coat

memes.label: "The Carl of the Drab Coat (Tale)"
memes.description: "The Carl of the Drab Coat is the seventh tale in James Stephens' Irish Fairy Tales, following Fionn mac Uail's strange journey through Faery and his encounter with a mysterious shape-shifting giant in a drab coat."
aliases: The Carl of the Drab Coat
Carl of the Drab Coat tale

relations.label: The Carl of the Drab Coat

memes.label: "Cesh Corran"
memes.description: "Cesh Corran is a hill in Connacht topped by a hunting-mound of the Fianna, and the site of a sídhe dwelling ruled by Conaran son of Imidel — an entrance to Faery where Fionn was once captured by enchanted hags."

memes.label: "The Enchanted Cave of Cesh Corran — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "Fionn mac Uail was the most prudent chief of an army in the world, but"

memes.label: "The Enchanted Cave of Cesh Corran — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "Now the king of the Shi’ of Cesh Corran, Conaran, son of Imidel, was"

memes.label: "The Enchanted Cave of Cesh Corran — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "BY his arts Conaran changed the sight of Fionn’s eyes, and he did the"

memes.label: "The Enchanted Cave of Cesh Corran — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "The instant they passed the holly a strange weakness came over the"

memes.label: "The Enchanted Cave of Cesh Corran — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "Within the cave there was silence except for the voices of the hags and"

memes.label: "The Enchanted Cave of Cesh Corran — CHAPTER VI"
memes.description: "As each man came out of the cave he gave a jump and a shout; the courage"

memes.label: Clann Morna
memes.description: The rival warrior clan of the Fianna, led by Goll mac Morna, who killed Fionn mac Uail's father Cumhal and hunted the young hero through his boyhood, representing the southern (Leath Mogha) faction of the warrior elite.

memes.label: "Conan mac Morna"
memes.description: "Conan mac Morna, called Cona'n the Swearer or Bald Cona'n, is the gruff, combative brother of Goll mac Morna, whose sharp tongue and quick fists help ignite the Brawl at Allen."

memes.label: "Cona'n the Swearer"
memes.description: "Cona'n the Swearer is a member of the Fianna, present with Fionn at the cave of Cesh Corran where the party was ensnared by Conaran and his daughters. Known for his characteristic oaths and exclamations, he represents the loyal but fallible warrior companion in the Fenian cycle."

memes.label: "Conaran son of Imidel"
memes.description: "Conaran, son of Imidel, was the king of the Shi' of Cesh Corran, a Tuatha De Danann lord whose magical arts ensnared Fionn and the Fianna in one of the Fenian cycle's most memorable supernatural encounters."

memes.label: "Conn of the Hundred Battles"
memes.description: "Conn of the Hundred Battles, also called Conn the Hundred Fighter, is the Ard-Ri (High King) of Ireland in the time of Fionn mac Uail. He sits at Tara and appears as a central monarchical figure in the tales of Becuma of the White Skin."

memes.label: Connacht
memes.description: "The western province of Ireland, home of the wild Connachtmen of the Fianna, the sídhe mound of Cruachan Ahi, and the fierce warrior Goll Mac Morna."

memes.label: "Cormac mac Art"
memes.description: "Cormac mac Art is the High King of Ireland who presides over the judgement at Tara following the Brawl at Allen, a figure of legal authority and measured wisdom in the Fenian Cycle."

memes.label: Crimthann mac Ae
memes.description: Crimthann, son of Ae, King of Leinster, the favourite foster-son of the High King Dermod mac Ae at Tara. A young prince known for his eagerness to learn, modesty, and close companionship with the High King.

memes.label: Cumhal mac Art
memes.description: Cumhal mac Art (also known as Uail mac Baiscne in the Stephens text), father of Fionn mac Uail, was the leader of the Fianna before his death at the hands of Clann Morna.

memes.label: "Daire Degamra"
memes.description: "Daire Degamra was the king of a paradisal island beyond Ireland, husband of the daughter of Lodan and father of Segda, whose hospitality to Conn of the Hundred Battles set the stage for the geasa crisis of Becuma's tale."

memes.label: Delvcaem
memes.description: Delvcaem (Fair Shape), daughter of the great magician Morgan, a captive princess of the Many-Coloured Land whom Art mac Conn sought and won in the tale of Becuma of the White Skin.

memes.label: "Dog Heads"
memes.description: "The Dog Heads are a race of dog-headed beings inhabiting the Irish Otherworld, ruled by a king and connected to the supernatural challenges that heroes must overcome in the tales."

memes.label: Donegal
memes.description: "Northern region in Ulster where Finnian, the Abbot of Moville, first heard news of remaining pagans, setting in motion his journey to confront Tuan Mac Cairill."

memes.label: Druid
memes.description: The learned priestly class of pre-Christian Ireland, possessing powers of prophecy, magic, and poetic wisdom in Irish mythology.

memes.label: Dún
memes.description: A fortified stronghold or royal seat in early Irish society, serving as the dwelling-place of kings, chieftains, and warriors, frequently appearing as a setting for mythological events.

relations.label: the Carl of the Drab Coat

memes.label: "Duv Laca"
memes.description: "Duv Laca of the White Hand is the wife of Mongan mac Fiachna, a proud and spirited queen whose abduction by the King of Leinster drives the central conflict of 'Mongan's Frenzy.'"
aliases: Duv Laca of the White Hand

memes.label: Enchantment
memes.description: Magical enchantment (druidecht) in Irish mythology—the supernatural art of spells, transformations, and illusion practiced by druids and the Tuatha Dé Danann.

memes.label: Ethal Anbual
memes.description: A Tuatha Dé Danann deity who presides over the Connacht underworld at Cruachan Ahi, named in the corpus as a lord of the sídhe realm beneath the royal site of Connacht.

memes.label: "Faery"
memes.description: "Faery is the supernatural Otherworld of Irish mythology — a layered realm comprising the Shi', the Many-Coloured Land, the Land of Wonder, and the Land of Promise, accessible only through magic and home to the Tuatha De Danann and the Sidhe."

memes.label: Fear Doirche
memes.description: Fear Doirche, the Dark Druid, is a malevolent sorcerer of the Tuatha Dé Danann and the primary antagonist of the Oisin's Mother tale, known for abducting women and wielding a hazel rod of transformation.

memes.label: Feast of Moy Life
memes.description: The Feast of Moy Life (Feis Magh Life), a great assembly and celebration held on the plain of the Liffey in Leinster, scene of both Fionn's naming and Mongan's wanderings, drawing all ranks of Irish society together in festivity.

memes.label: "Feast of Tara"
memes.description: "The Feast of Tara is the great annual assembly at Samhain when the High King hosts all the Fianna of Ireland — a convergence of warriors, poets, and nobles that serves as the social and political heart of the mythological year."

memes.label: Fergus Fionnliath
memes.description: Fergus Fionnliath, a man notorious for his hatred of dogs, whose stronghold lay near the harbour of Galway. His antipathy to dogs made him the perfect instrument for Uct Dealv's revenge against Tuiren.

memes.label: "Fergus True-Lips"
memes.description: "Fergus True-Lips, also known as Fergus Fionnliath, is the chief poet of the Fianna, renowned for his hatred of dogs, his powerful incantatory verse, and his role as a truth-teller at the judgement following the Brawl at Allen."

memes.label: "Fiachna Finn"
memes.description: "Fiachna Finn, son of Baltan, was an Ulster king elected to the high kingship and the mortal father of Mongan — his rivalry with Fiachna Duv and his death in battle set in motion the events of Mongan's Frenzy."

memes.label: "Fiachna Duv"
memes.description: "Fiachna Duv mac Demain was a prince of the Dal Fiatach, the rival and enemy of Fiachna Finn, and the father of Duv Laca of the White Hand in the tale of Mongan's Frenzy."

relations.label: Duv Laca of the White Hand

memes.label: "Fiachna Finn"
memes.description: "Fiachna Finn mac Baltan was a King of Ulster, the mortal father of Mongan, and a central figure in the early chapters of Mongan's Frenzy."

memes.label: Fiacuil mac Cona
memes.description: A great robber and warrior who killed poets, married Bovmall (Fionn's aunt), and ran a fighting school in the wilds of Slieve Bloom where the young Fionn trained.

memes.label: "Fianna"
memes.description: "The Fianna is the legendary warrior-band of Irish mythology, the standing army of the High Kings of Ireland, led by Fionn mac Uail and composed of the greatest heroes of the Fenian Cycle — hunters, poets, and warriors gathered under a single banner."

memes.label: "Finegas"
memes.description: "Finegas is the druid and poet who serves as Fionn mac Uail's tutor on the bank of the Boyne River, and the destined catcher of the Salmon of Knowledge whose accidental consumption by Fionn grants the boy all the wisdom of the world."

memes.label: Finnian
memes.description: Abbott of Moville who journeyed to confront the last pagan holdouts in Donegal and, through patient siege, won Tuan mac Cairill's conversion and recorded his ancient life story.

memes.label: "Fintan"
memes.description: "Fintan mac Bocna was one of the most ancient beings in Irish mythology — a survivor of the Flood who lived for thousands of years, serving as a keeper of primordial memory and a judge whose wisdom spanned the ages of the world."

memes.label: "Fionn mac Uail"
memes.description: "Fionn mac Uail is the central hero of the Fenian Cycle, leader of the Fianna of Ireland, who gained supernatural wisdom from the Salmon of Knowledge."

memes.label: "Flame Lady"
memes.description: "The Flame Lady, also known as Bro'tiarna, was the passionate and fierce wife of Mongan, King of Ulster, whose fiery temperament gave her the epithet by which she is known in Irish mythology."

memes.label: "Flann"
memes.description: "Flann was a young warrior in the tale of Mongan's Frenzy whose love affair with Dubh Laca, the wife of Mongan, became the vehicle through which Mongan demonstrated the power of shapeshifting and supernatural deception."

memes.label: Fosterage
memes.description: The Irish fosterage system (altram), a social institution where children were raised by foster-families to strengthen clan alliances and provide specialized training.

memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "1. CHAPTER I"

memes.label: Geasa
memes.description: "Geasa (singular: geis)—sacred taboos, prohibitions, and supernatural obligations binding kings and heroes in Irish mythology. Violation led to inevitable doom, and the most powerful Irish narratives are built on the tragic conflicts between competing geasa."

memes.label: "Goll mac Morna"
memes.description: "Goll mac Morna is the formidable one-eyed leader of clann-Morna, rival and eventual ally of Fionn mac Uail, known for his immense strength, mirthful disposition, and commanding presence in the Fianna."

memes.label: Hazel Rod
memes.description: The hazel rod is a magical instrument of transformation and enchantment wielded by sorcerers of the Tuatha Dé Danann, used to change the form of living beings against their will.

memes.label: Iollan Eachtach
memes.description: An Ulster gentleman and member of the Fianna who married Tuiren, Fionn's sister, but whose past love affair with the sidhe-woman Uct Dealv brought tragedy upon his bride.

memes.label: Ivell of the Shining Cheeks
memes.description: The illusory identity given by Mongan to the Hag of the Mill through shape-changing magic, presented as the daughter of the King of Munster and wife of Ae the Beautiful, used to trick King Branduv of Leinster and recover Duv Laca.

memes.label: Kerry
memes.description: "The southwestern region of Ireland, where Fionn's mother Muirne was fostered, the King of Kerry employed Fionn, and Bovmall and Lia Luachra raised the infant Fionn in secret."

memes.label: "Kings and Chieftains"
memes.description: "A comprehensive listing of kings, high kings, chieftains, and rulers from Irish mythology as depicted in James Stephens' Irish Fairy Tales."

memes.label: Land of Promise
memes.description: The Land of Promise (Tir Tairngire), the Otherworld paradise of Irish mythology where the Tuatha Dé Danann dwelt, a realm of eternal youth, wisdom, and supernatural beauty.

memes.label: "Leinster"
memes.description: "Leinster is one of the five ancient provinces of Ireland, ruled during the era of the Fenian tales by King Branduv. It features prominently in Mongan's Frenzy as the setting for the tale's central trick and exchange."

memes.label: Lia Luachra
memes.description: A druid-woman of Slieve Luachra in Kerry who, together with Bovmall, fostered the infant Fionn mac Uail in hiding, protecting the future leader of the Fianna from his enemies during his boyhood.

memes.label: "Lochlann"
memes.description: "Lochlann, the land of the Northmen (Lochlannachs), was both a realm of hostile invaders from across the sea and, in its 'White Lochlann' aspect, a kingdom that Fionn conquered, where his father Cumhal fell in battle."

memes.label: "Lugh"
memes.description: "Lugh Lámhfada, divine hero and High King of the Tuatha Dé Danann, appears in the corpus as the brother of Ethlinn and a god whose mere kinship should have protected his mortal relatives from harm."

memes.label: Mac an Dáv
memes.description: A loyal servant and companion of Mongan, known for carrying his master on his back from Ulster and for his plain-spoken, humorous manner.

memes.label: "Manannán mac Lir"
memes.description: "Manannán mac Lir is the sea god of the Tuatha Dé Danann, ruler of the Otherworld isles, and a figure of immense mythological significance referenced briefly in Becuma's origin story."

memes.label: "Many-Coloured Land"
memes.description: "The Many-Coloured Land is a realm of the Irish Otherworld lying beyond the Shi' (Faery), accessible only by crossing water after passing through the clay-boundary of the sidhe mounds."

memes.label: "Meath"
memes.description: "Meath is the central province of ancient Ireland, home to Tara and the Hill of Uisneach, site of royal burials, councils, and the funeral games of Conn of the Hundred Battles' queen."

memes.label: "Milesians"
memes.description: "The Milesians are the mythological invading race who defeated the Tuatha De Danann and became the ancestors of the Irish people, according to the Lebor Gabála Érenn tradition."

memes.label: "Mongan"
memes.description: "Mongan is the son of Fiachna Finn and a prince of Ulster, secretly the reincarnation of Fionn mac Uail, whose frenzied pursuit of his wife Duv Laca drives the tale of Mongan's Frenzy."

relations.label: Duv Laca of the White Hand

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "The abbot of the Monastery of Moville sent word to the story-tellers of"

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "Said Cairide’:"

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "In the year when Ciaran the son of the Carpenter died, the same year"

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "There is a difference between this world and the world of Faery, but it"

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "Said Mongan:"

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER VI"
memes.description: "A year passed, and one day as he was sitting at judgement there came"

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER VII"
memes.description: "“It is not nice of you to laugh at us,” said Fiachna Finn."

memes.label: "Mongan's Frenzy — CHAPTER VIII"
memes.description: "“You have not told me one word about Duv Laca,” said the Flame Lady"

memes.description: "THEY sat in a place where they could watch the castle and compose"

memes.description: "The doorkeeper brought word to the King of Leinster that the son of the"

memes.label: Moville
memes.description: "A monastery whose abbot, Finnian, sets out to confront paganism and later collects the stories of Ireland, appearing in both The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill and Mongan's Frenzy."

memes.label: Muirne
memes.description: Beautiful, long-haired mother of Fionn mac Uail, who hid her son from the vengeance of Clann Morna and later married the King of Kerry.

memes.label: Munster
memes.description: "The southern province of Ireland, home to the King of Munster's daughter Ivell, the Slieve Luachra mountains, and a Munster lord encountered on the road."

memes.label: Nemed mac Agnoman
memes.description: Nemed, son of Agnoman, leader of the second legendary invasion of Ireland. After Partholon's race perished, Nemed arrived with a fleet of thirty-four barques and his people flourished before being scattered by a mysterious catastrophe.

memes.label: Nuada
memes.description: "Nuada Airgetlám (Nuada of the Silver Hand), first king of the Tuatha Dé Danann, appears in the Stephens corpus as an ancestor of Becuma of the White Skin, dwelling in Faery."

memes.label: Oisin
memes.description: Oisin, son of Fionn mac Uail and Saeve, is a celebrated warrior-poet of the Fianna, known for his eloquence and his mysterious birth from an enchanted mother.

memes.label: "Oisin's Mother — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "EVENING was drawing nigh, and the Fianna-Finn had decided to hunt no"

memes.label: "Oisin's Mother — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "Late that night, when he was preparing for rest, the door of Fionn’s"

memes.label: "Oisin's Mother — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "It happened that the men of Lochlann came on an expedition against"

memes.label: "Oisin's Mother — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "“When you had been away for a day the guards were surprised. They were"

memes.label: "Oisin's Mother — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "For many years after that time, when he was not fighting against the"

memes.label: "Oisin's Mother — CHAPTER VI"
memes.description: "Just as at one time he could not be parted from Saeve, so now he could"

memes.label: "Oisin's Mother — CHAPTER VII"
memes.description: "“I used to live,” he said, “in a wide, beautiful place. There were hills"

memes.label: "Oscar"
memes.description: "Oscar, son of Oisin and grandson of Fionn mac Uail, is a fierce warrior of the Fianna who fights in the Brawl at Allen and later appears alongside Fionn and Oisin in the adventures of the Carl of the Drab Coat."

memes.label: Partholon
memes.description: Partholon, the legendary invader who led the first settlement of Ireland after the Great Flood. A descendant of Noah, he arrived with twenty-four couples and founded the first civilization in Ireland, only to have his entire race wiped out by plague within a single week.

memes.label: Prophecy
memes.description: The practice and belief in prophetic foreknowledge in Irish mythology, governing the fates of heroes through oracular declarations and destined events.

memes.label: Ráth
memes.description: "The ráth — the circular earthen ringfort of early medieval Ireland — was the standard fortified dwelling of the free landholding class, and its most famous example, Tara of the Kings, appears throughout the Stephens corpus as the ceremonial heart of Ireland."

memes.label: "Rath Cruachan"
memes.description: "Rath Cruachan is the sidhe-mound of Connacht, the supernatural dwelling ruled by the Carl of the Drab Coat, a jovial giant of the Shi'."

memes.label: "Saeve"
memes.description: "Saeve, a woman of Faery, was the beloved of Fionn mac Uail and the mother of Oisin. Pursued by the Fear Doirche, she was transformed into a fawn and known as the Flower of Allen."

memes.label: "Salmon of Knowledge"
memes.description: "The Salmon of Knowledge is the legendary fish of Irish mythology whose flesh conferred all the wisdom of the world upon whoever ate it — a gift that fell to the young Fionn mac Uail through accident, revealing itself as destiny."

memes.label: Samhain
memes.description: The great seasonal festival marking the end of the harvest and the beginning of winter in Irish tradition, when the boundary between the mortal world and the Otherworld grows thin.

memes.label: "Sceolan"
memes.description: "Sceolan was one of Fionn mac Uail's two legendary wolfhounds, alongside his brother Bran, born of a Sidhe enchantment."

memes.label: "Segda"
memes.description: "Segda (Sweet Speech), son of Daire Degamra, was the youth who broke the geas-impasse between the High King Conn and the island queen by agreeing to eat with the king, and later stood trial in the debate over Becuma's fate."

memes.label: "Shapeshifting"
memes.description: "Shapeshifting is a pervasive supernatural power in Irish mythology — the ability of gods, druids, and enchanted beings to change form across species and across the boundaries between human, animal, and elemental existence."

memes.label: Sídhe Mound
memes.description: The Otherworld dwelling-mound of the Tuatha Dé Danann, a supernatural abode existing beneath the earth, accessible at liminal times such as Samhain.

memes.label: "Slieve Bloom"
memes.description: "The woods of Slieve Bloom are the secret wilderness refuge where the infant Fionn mac Uail was hidden from his father's enemies, the sons of Morna, and where he was raised in safety by the women who protected him."

memes.label: Slieve Luachra
memes.description: "A mountain range in Munster, sixty miles from the Hill of the Rushes, where the Carl of the Drab Coat and his companion journey and spend a night in the tale."

memes.label: "Supernatural Law"
memes.description: "Supernatural law in Irish mythology encompasses the binding taboos (geasa), sacred obligations, and divine prohibitions that govern the conduct of kings, heroes, and supernatural beings, whose violation invites catastrophe and cosmic disorder."

memes.label: "Tailltin"
memes.description: "Tailltin, in Royal Meath, was the sacred place where Conn of the Hundred Battles laid his wife to rest and held nine days of funeral games, establishing a tradition that would endure in Irish lore."

memes.label: "Tales of Irish Fairy Tales"
memes.description: "A complete index of all ten tales in James Stephens' Irish Fairy Tales (1920), with chapter counts, key characters, and narrative summaries."

memes.label: "Tara"
memes.description: "Tara is the sacred hill and royal seat of the High Kings of Ireland, where feasts are held, judgements are rendered, and the fate of the land is decided — the axis where mortal kingship meets supernatural authority in Irish mythology."

memes.label: "The Carl"
memes.description: "The Carl of the Drab Coat is a supernatural being of the sidhe who appears as a monstrous yellow-skinned giant to defend Ireland's honour against the Thessalian champion Cael of the Iron."
aliases: The Carl of the Drab Coat
Ruler of the Shi' of Rath Cruachan

relations.label: The Carl of the Drab Coat — Chapter 1

memes.label: Thumb of Knowledge
memes.description: "The Thumb of Knowledge (Ordóg an Eolais) was the supernatural gift Fionn mac Uail acquired from the Salmon of Knowledge — by sucking his burned thumb, he could access hidden wisdom, foresight, and the answers to any question."

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER I"
memes.description: "Finnian, the Abbott of Moville, went southwards and eastwards in great"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER II"
memes.description: "Now Finnian could not abide that any person should resist both the"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER III"
memes.description: "One day they were talking together about the majesty of God and His"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER IV"
memes.description: "But Finnian was not one who remained long in bewilderment. He thought on"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER V"
memes.description: "“Tell on, my love,” Finnian murmured"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER VI"
memes.description: "“THERE I dreamed, and I saw myself changing into a stag in dream, and"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER VII"
memes.description: "“Then my soul rose to the height of Doom, and I intended all that might"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER VIII"
memes.description: "“OLD age again overtook me. Weariness stole into my limbs, and anguish"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER IX"
memes.description: "“I became the king of the salmon, and, with my multitudes, I ranged on"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER X"
memes.description: "“Delight and strength came to me again, and now I explored all the"

memes.label: "The Story of Tuan Mac Cairill — CHAPTER XI"
memes.description: "“THE fisherman of Cairill, the King of Ulster, took me in his net. Ah,"

memes.label: "Tuan Mac Cairill"
memes.description: "Tuan Mac Cairill is the immortal shapeshifter of Irish mythology who witnessed all the invasions of Ireland from the time of Partholon to the coming of Christianity, transforming through animal forms — stag, boar, hawk, and salmon — before being reborn as the son of Cairill, King of Ulster."

memes.label: "Tuatha De Danann"
memes.description: "The Tuatha De Danann are the divine race of Irish mythology — godlike beings who ruled Ireland before the coming of the Milesians and who retreated into the Sidhe mounds to become the People of Faery, the supernatural source from which mortal heroes draw their heritage."

memes.label: Tuiren
memes.description: The sister of Muirne and aunt of Fionn mac Uail, transformed into a hound by a jealous rival of the Sídhe and mother of the legendary wolfhounds Bran and Sceólan.

memes.label: "Uct Dealv"
memes.description: "Uct Dealv (Fair Breast) is a woman of the Shi' (sidhe), the fairy lover of Cairide and a key figure in the events that set The Wooing of Becfola in motion."

memes.label: Ulster
memes.description: "The northern province of Ireland, home to Cairill the King, Iollan Eachtach, and the cave-dwelling of Tuan Mac Cairill during his animal transformations."

memes.label: "Warrior Code"
memes.description: "The warrior code of the Fianna is the unwritten system of honour, loyalty, martial skill, and ethical conduct that governs the warrior-bands of Irish mythology, transmitted through fosterage, poetry, and example rather than written law."

memes.label: Well of Segais
memes.description: The Well of Segais (Connla's Well), a sacred pool overhung by the nine Hazel trees of wisdom whose nuts fell into the water and were eaten by the Salmon of Knowledge.

memes.label: Wide Allen of Leinster
memes.description: The Wide Allen of Leinster (Mag nAlmaine Laigen) was the great plain in Leinster where the Hill of Allen stood and the Fianna held their seat of power. One of the most geographically significant locations in the Fenian cycle, it served as the home territory and training ground for Ireland's legendary warrior-band.

memes.label: Wooing of Becfola
memes.description: The wooing and marriage of Becfola the Dowerless, a mysterious woman of unknown origin who became the wife of the High King at Tara, set during the Feast of Samhain.
